summary: Daily Hacker News product scout: nine new concrete projects, products, and tools selected after broad front-page comment coverage.
telegram_footer: 9 items surfaced | 25/25 stories scraped | history + run artifact updated

items.name: Frame
items.source_story_title: Frame – Linux X server in Assembly
items.summary: A Linux X server implemented in x86-64 assembly language.
items.why_included: A striking systems project with a direct, readable build write-up.
items.evidence: HN discussion examined its tiny implementation and the trade-offs of building an X server in assembly.

items.name: Everything
items.source_story_title: Show HN: A zoomable timeline of 4M Wikipedia events
items.summary: A zoomable visual timeline for exploring four million Wikipedia events.
items.why_included: A concrete exploration interface that offers a novel way to browse historical data.
items.evidence: HN commenters discussed the scale and interactive timeline presentation of the Show HN project.

items.name: Honeypot Live
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Watch bots interact with an SSH honeypot in real time
items.summary: A live visual feed of bots interacting with an SSH honeypot.
items.why_included: It makes otherwise invisible background internet noise immediately tangible.
items.evidence: HN commenters called the project fun to watch and discussed privacy-safe ways to enrich the live telemetry.

items.name: Workspaces
items.source_story_title: Workspaces – Explore the workspaces of modern creators
items.summary: An archive of creator workspaces, gear lists, and interviews.
items.why_included: The six-year, 500-plus interview archive is a useful source of real setup ideas.
items.evidence: HN commenters praised the archive while pushing it toward less staged, more representative workspaces.

items.name: Pebble
items.summary: The revived Pebble brand building e-paper watches and a smart ring.
items.why_included: The hardware update surfaced concrete new devices and candid early-production discussion.
items.evidence: HN discussion covered Pebble Time 2 and Index 01 details, especially the smart ring’s sizing and battery design.

items.name: So Far
items.summary: A browser demo of fast homomorphic-encrypted image classification.
items.why_included: It is a notable hands-on demonstration of private inference performance.
items.evidence: HN commenters verified the demo’s 200ms claim context while flagging that its shared demo keys are not a production trust model.

items.name: Camera Chase Vehicle
items.summary: A meticulously documented DIY camera rover with a stabilized gimbal.
items.why_included: A high-signal maker build that prioritizes practical detail over product hype.
items.evidence: HN commenters praised the exhaustive build log, engineering choices, and playful physical design.

items.name: VulnHunter
items.source_story_title: VulnHunter: Capital One's agentic AI code security tool
items.summary: An open-source agentic workflow for code security assessments.
items.why_included: A reusable, concrete security-assessment methodology released in public.
items.evidence: HN commenters described it as an open-sourced methodology, debated its differentiation, and linked its public repository.

items.name: Babashka
items.source_story_title: A Road to Lisp: Which Lisp
items.summary: A fast scripting runtime for Clojure on the JVM ecosystem.
items.why_included: A direct community recommendation for practical Clojure scripting and developer ergonomics.
items.evidence: HN commenters specifically recommended Babashka and praised its REPL-driven scripting workflow.
